steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The sentence uses the correlative conjunction pair "not only... but also." The rule: no punctuation should interrupt either half of this pair. The blank fills the first element: "caused ______ by cyclones but also by vapor streams."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "caused not only by cyclones—": places a single dash after "cyclones," but there is no closing dash later in the sentence to complete the pair. An unpaired dash is a punctuation error. ✗
  • "caused—not only by cyclones—": wraps "not only by cyclones" inside paired dashes, treating it as a parenthetical insert. But "not only by cyclones" is not parenthetical — it is one half of the correlative structure and cannot be removed without breaking the sentence. ✗
  • "caused, not only by cyclones,": wraps the same phrase in commas, which has the same flaw as B: it isolates a non-parenthetical element. ✗
  • "caused not only by cyclones": no internal punctuation interrupts the correlative pair; the full structure reads cleanly: "may be caused not only by cyclones but also by vapor streams." ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
